title: Chapter Twenty-Four: Blood and Signboards
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The sect leader's corpse brought silence.

The ominous smiles of the Praying Mantis Sect disciples vanished. The tremendous rising blood-scent made the surreal death feel real.

Yeon-shin turned away from the ruin of the man and held the Desolate Sword lowered, blood dripping from it.

"Today, I will destroy the signboard of the Praying Mantis Sect."

"Wh-what?"

"Cr-crazy--!"

He rode the momentum. Did not give them time to think.

Step.

As he took one step forward, many cowards stepped back. The loyalty of evil sect martial artists was different from the faith of wandering swordsmen. They were people who bought and sold martial arts. Life could be exchanged for even a penny.

"Kill him!"

"You go first!"

"He's an invincible monster!"

"Di-divine sword--!"

*Monster and divine sword. Words I'd never hear in Desolate Sect.*

He wedged through the loosened sword net and delivered swift sword strikes. Some started to run.

"Bl-blood sword! Let's call the blood sword!"

Everyone in the courtyard paused. Including Yeon-shin.

*Blood sword. The Blood Swordmaster. The Blood Flame Sect was behind this.*

He grabbed one man's arm with a joint lock, held the blue blade to his neck.

"Answer. Was the Blood Flame Sect behind this? Did you offer the people you took to them too?"

"Ye-yes! They should be sleeping in the annex now! Th-that's how it always was!"

"You don't know high-level masters."

How could they not have sensed a fight of this level?

He raised the Desolate Sword. The true energy of the Fate Conquering Scripture filled the blood vessels of his right arm, waist, and lower body.

A surprise sword strike hit the Desolate Sword. No sound of split air. Only reddish hair brushing past Yeon-shin's forehead.

Blood Swordmaster.

The vibration through his hand was of a completely different order from anyone in the Praying Mantis Sect.

"You blocked that?" the Blood Swordmaster said, red and black hair tangled, his mouth stained with old blood. "Then this carnage must be your doing."

"Carnage. That's not a word for the Blood Flame Sect to use."

The same as the one he had seen in Jinpyeong County. The same ominous energy. The iron sword in his hand, the bright red martial uniform.

Ma Jin's words came to mind. *Cut down about five Blood Swordmasters, and you'll be recognised as a Blue Warrior regardless of age.* A joke, he had thought.

"Lucky," Yeon-shin said.

"What?"

"Three left."

The Blood Swordmaster's eyes narrowed. He was a rare master who could sense Yeon-shin's energy, and what he sensed made him wary.

Yeon-shin did not wait.

Power imbued in the leg that struck the ground. The heavy resonance of the Radiant Demon Swift Sword Technique flashed in a straight line, colliding with the Blood Swordmaster's iron blade. Initiative taken. Force properly applied. Clearly pushed back.

*End it here. This is the heart of the Praying Mantis Sect. The longer the fight, the worse my odds.*

His sky-blue eyes flashed, sweeping the enemy's entire body. The sense honed through group battle saw through to behind the man -- piercing even the energy gathering in his left hand.

*Here.*

Instinct. He chose to reach first rather than clash martial arts. The decision turned into energy layered around the Desolate Sword. The foot force of the Eternal Blossom Fist's Spiral imbued into the Radiant Demon Swift Sword Technique. Sword unification. Space folded.

The blade went in with a small, precise force.

The Blood Swordmaster's ominous energy scattered. His eyes widened, tearing with disbelief.

"You--"

"I don't listen to last words."

His arm muscles visibly rose. He struck the embedded sword to the side while turning. The body fell without a groan.

He turned to the remaining disciples, who were trying to slink away.

"Don't flee."

They stopped.

---

Yeon-shin shattered the signboard of the Praying Mantis Sect in front of the people of Lianhu County.

Some rejoiced, stomping on the split wood. Others burst into tears, holding the emaciated corpses of their families. Over a dozen bodies had been discovered in the annex and backyard.

*The same as Jinpyeong County.*

Jang-sam stood beside him, wiping tears. He had wrapped his daughter's corpse in cloth and carried it on his back.

"Xi'an should be the territory of Zhongnan," Yeon-shin murmured. "What have they been doing?"

"Isn't the Zhongnan Sect at war with the Tyrant Sword Sect?" Jang-sam said, his voice slurred and bloodshot.

Everything stopped.

"Zhongnan, with the Tyrant Sword Sect?"

A rumour he had never heard. His nephew's face flashed before him. The Zhongnan Sect masters. And at the end, the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader -- the one who had annihilated his family while revealing the aura of an absolute being.

"Since when?"

"I'm not sure. I only heard the nearby Zhongnan-affiliated martial halls packed their bags and went up to Zhongnan Mountain..."

Yeon-shin fell silent. If the opponent was the Tyrant Sword Sect, the Zhongnan Sect would have to risk everything. It would not have ended with one or two masters dying. Elder Yeo Il-sin's ear had been cut off by the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader. The annihilation of the Jeong family. The unnatural presence of the Zhongnan Sect masters at his family's house that night.

Little Hyea. His nephew. He hoped Jeong Jung-san was protecting her properly.

*I need to go to Zhongnan.*

Ma Jin would allow it. The Thirteen Heavens were forces that acted as the supreme in the evil sect martial world. They were bound to be targets of Desolate Sect's military intervention, and beheading Tyrant Sword Sect masters was directly accumulating achievements.

*I can gain achievements. And now I need to know. Why the Jeong family fell.*

The door inside him rattled. He kept it shut.

He received the villagers' gratitude. Refused the feast, telling them to add the food to the funerals. Watched people mutter his name like a prayer.

At the top of the hill crossing Lianhu County, he spoke without turning.

"Did you find traces of Thieving Ghost?"

The bushes shook. Cheon Ju and Yu Hyeon revealed themselves.

"It doesn't seem to have been the Praying Mantis Sect," Cheon Ju said. Perhaps because he had witnessed the annihilation, his words were much more respectful now. The attitude of facing a true martial artist, not a promising child.

"I see. Unfortunate."

"I was impressed by your prowess. As if looking at the future leader of Radiant Demon Wing -- no, the Divine Sword Leader."

Cheon Ju patted the back of Yu Hyeon's head. The Taoist crown shook. Yu Hyeon's impression, which had looked languid before, now seemed clear.

"It was my first defeat," Yu Hyeon said. "When I think of the five stances I mentioned, I want to fall off the bed."

"First defeat?"

"My senior brothers and junior brothers are all much older. The only peers are the outer sect disciples, but their martial arts are too shallow." He slightly lowered his eyes. "How about we form a friendship? You seem interested in Mount Hua martial arts. Shaanxi isn't far from Hebei."

"I won't have any reason to go to Mount Hua on purpose."

"Oh..."

"If you come to find me, I won't refuse. But each time, learn a new martial art."

Yu Hyeon's expression brightened despite the rejection. On the way to Xi'an, Yeon-shin had to listen to the young Taoist's chatter -- mainly about the principles of martial arts, the orthodox philosophy of the Nine Sects. Different from Heon Won-chang. Helpful.

The occasional words added by Cheon Ju were on the verge of becoming inspirations.

*I am looking forward to Mount Hua's visit.*

They arrived at the inn. The good times ended there.

He went up to Ma Jin's room and laid out the sack he had been carrying.

Two severed heads rolled out. Red and black hair. The split signboard of the Praying Mantis Sect clattered beside them.

"Oh!"

"...!"

Heon Won-chang went white. Baek Mi-ryeo's eyes sharpened. Ma Jin's scar darkened.

Proof. Achievement. And a request.

"I want to climb Mount Zhongnan," Yeon-shin said.

---
